content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Applied all 3 pending amendments: (1) hoisted error.to_string() above the journal event in engine.rs so the string is computed once and shared between the journal event and runtime log, avoiding a redundant allocation on large error messages; (2) added bounded stderr_tail (STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT) to all 3 Codex failure paths for consistency with Claude paths — invalid last-message JSON, missing last-message with stdout fallback, and generic read-failure; (3) added bounded payload snippets to all schema-validation failure errors so the actual malformed content is visible in the runtime log: stdout_tail for Claude envelope parse failures and empty-result failures, result_tail for Claude result JSON parse failures, and message_tail for Codex invalid last-message JSON failures. Updated 3 existing tests to verify the new fields (stdout_tail, result_tail, message_tail, stderr_tail).

## Steps

1. [Completed] Hoist error.to_string() into error_display above the journal event construction in engine.rs, pass &error_display to both journal::stage_failed_event and the runtime log format, eliminating the duplicate .to_string() call
2. [Completed] Add bounded stderr_tail (STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT) to the Codex 'invalid last-message JSON' error path (process_backend.rs ~line 315)
3. [Completed] Add bounded stderr_tail to the Codex 'missing last-message, stdout fallback invalid' error path (process_backend.rs ~line 330)
4. [Completed] Add bounded stderr_tail to the Codex 'failed to read last-message file' generic error path (process_backend.rs ~line 345)
5. [Completed] Add bounded stdout_tail to the Claude 'invalid envelope JSON' error path so the actual malformed stdout is visible in the runtime log
6. [Completed] Add bounded result_tail (tail of envelope.result) to the Claude 'invalid result JSON' error path so the actual bad payload is visible
7. [Completed] Add bounded stdout_tail to the Claude 'empty result with no structured_output' error path
8. [Completed] Add bounded message_tail (tail of last_message_text) to the Codex 'invalid last-message JSON' error path so the malformed file content is visible
9. [Completed] Update test finish_claude_invalid_envelope_includes_stdout_len_and_stderr to also assert stdout_tail presence and content
10. [Completed] Update test finish_claude_invalid_result_json_includes_stdout_len_and_stderr to also assert result_tail presence and content
11. [Completed] Update test finish_codex_invalid_last_message_includes_output_sizes to also assert message_tail and stderr_tail presence
12. [Completed] Run cargo fmt, cargo test (757 pass, 0 fail), cargo clippy (0 new warnings in modified files), cargo fmt --check (clean)

## Validation Evidence

- 590 lib tests pass (0 regressions); all 6 diagnostic-specific tests pass including updated assertions for stdout_tail, result_tail, message_tail
- 757 total tests pass across all test targets (0 failures)
- cargo fmt --check passes with no formatting issues
- cargo clippy shows 0 new warnings in modified files (engine.rs, process_backend.rs)

## Outstanding Risks

- Codex CLI has no equivalent --verbose flag; transient Codex failures still rely on captured stderr and runtime/failed/*.failed.raw artifacts for full diagnosis beyond the bounded snippets now included in error messages
- All payload/stderr snippets are bounded to STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT (512 bytes) via truncate_str_tail; very large payloads will be clipped but the full content is always in runtime/failed/<id>.failed.raw and journal.ndjson
